title: Fix serializer method field hidden unique together (#9700)
body:
Fix: SerializerMethodField as HiddenField in unique_together

Corrects an issue where a `SerializerMethodField` that shares its name
with a model field involved in a `unique_together` constraint (or
`UniqueConstraint`) could be incorrectly treated as a `HiddenField`.

This typically occurred if the underlying model field was nullable or had
a default value, and the `SerializerMethodField` was not being correctly
prioritized during the uniqueness metadata processing.

The `ModelSerializer.get_uniqueness_extra_kwargs` method has been updated
to ensure that if a field name corresponding to a model field in a
uniqueness constraint is explicitly declared as a `SerializerMethodField`
on the serializer, it is not converted into a `HiddenField`.

A test case has been added to `tests/test_model_serializer.py`
(`TestSerializerMethodFieldInUniqueTogether`) to verify this behavior.

step_plan.summary: Prevent `ModelSerializer` uniqueness processing from injecting a `HiddenField` that overwrites an explicitly declared `SerializerMethodField` whose name matches a model field in `unique_together`/`UniqueConstraint`, and add regression coverage to ensure the declared method field remains intact.

steps.operation: Guard uniqueness hidden-field injection when a name is explicitly declared as SerializerMethodField
steps.reason: `get_uniqueness_extra_kwargs` may decide a uniqueness-participating model field is â€śmissingâ€ť from the serializer and inject `HiddenField(default=...)` under that name. When the serializer explicitly declares a `SerializerMethodField` with the same name, that injection incorrectly overrides/breaks the declared field. Add a check against `declared_fields` (specifically `SerializerMethodField`) before adding to `hidden_fields` so declared method fields are never replaced.
steps.side_effects: Changes behavior for serializers that declare `SerializerMethodField` names matching `unique_together`/`UniqueConstraint` fields: those fields will remain read-only method fields and uniqueness validation may rely on other fields/validators. Ensure existing uniqueness behavior remains unchanged for truly-omitted fields. May require updating/adding tests that assert no HiddenField is created in this case.

steps.operation: Review and, if needed, adjust model-field mapping so declared method fields are not treated as model-backed fields
steps.reason: The bug is triggered because `SerializerMethodField` typically does not map to a model field and therefore is absent from `model_fields`, pushing uniqueness logic into the HiddenField-injection branch. Confirm `_get_model_fields` does not incorrectly include/exclude declared fields in a way that would reintroduce the problem, and ensure any mapping logic doesnâ€™t misclassify declared method fields as missing model fields for uniqueness processing.
steps.side_effects: Any change here can affect how `ModelSerializer` detects model-backed fields across the board (field generation, extra_kwargs application, uniqueness logic). Could impact serializers with renamed fields (`source=...`), read-only fields, or explicitly declared fields. Run the full serializer test suite to catch regressions.

steps.operation: Ensure HiddenField merge logic cannot override explicitly declared SerializerMethodField names
steps.reason: `get_fields` is the integration point where computed `hidden_fields` are merged into the final `fields` dict (commonly via `fields.update(hidden_fields)`). Verify/adjust merge ordering or filtering so even if `hidden_fields` contains a colliding key, it will not overwrite an already-declared field (especially `SerializerMethodField`). This acts as a defense-in-depth in addition to the `get_uniqueness_extra_kwargs` fix.
steps.side_effects: Altering merge precedence can change long-standing behavior where injected hidden fields might have overridden auto-generated fields. Must ensure legitimate HiddenField injection still occurs when the serializer truly lacks the field. May require adjusting or adding tests for hidden field injection ordering and uniqueness validation.
